title: [lichen_util.h:62-79] secure_zero word path skips final read-back for remainder
description:
Category not checked: Cryptographic cleanup completeness

Bug found: The word-aligned path in secure_zero() does a read-back (line 76) for the word writes, but the remainder loop (lines 83-85) has NO read-back verification. The final bytes written are only protected by the compiler_barrier(), not by actual read verification.

This is inconsistent - either all bytes need read-back verification (paranoid mode) or none do. The partial read-back gives false confidence that the zeroing was verified while leaving the tail bytes unverified.

Impact: Minor - the compiler_barrier() should be sufficient, but the inconsistent treatment suggests incomplete reasoning about the security model.
close_reason: Fixed: Added comment explaining volatile qualifier handles security, no read-back needed
